faq.title: Frequently Asked Question
faq.clarityDescription: Tap another category or question to discover more about Certify's validation flow, security, and web3-native design.
faq.subtitle: Browse common questions about Certify, verification, and web3 trust, and learn how our secure verification experience works.

general.question: What is Certify?
general.answer: Certify is a blockchain-native certificate verification platform that makes authenticity checks instant, secure, and transparent. It provides a fast, user-friendly experience for anyone needing to confirm credentials without manual paperwork.

general.question: How does verification work?
general.answer: Our system verifies certificates by checking the digital record against an immutable blockchain registry, ensuring the credential is valid and unaltered. This means every verification is backed by a tamper-proof proof record.

general.question: Is my data private?
general.answer: Yes. Certify focuses on secure proof validation while keeping personal data private with encrypted registry lookups and minimal on-chain metadata. Only the information required for validation is used.

general.question: What types of certificates can be verified?
general.answer: Certify supports various credentials including academic degrees, professional certifications, training completion certificates, and digital skill badges issued across different institutions. It can support records from schools, employers, and credential issuers worldwide.

general.question: How do I get started with Certify?
general.answer: Simply navigate to our validation page, enter your certificate ID or upload your PDF, and our system will instantly verify its authenticity against our secure blockchain registry. No special software is required to get started.

security.question: Is the certificate data stored on chain?
security.answer: Only proof hashes and verification metadata are stored on chain. Sensitive certificate details remain off-chain to preserve privacy while maintaining integrity, and this design keeps personal data protected while preserving a secure audit trail.

security.question: Can certificates be tampered with?
security.answer: No. Once a certificate is issued and hashed on the blockchain, any modification will fail verification because the on-chain proof no longer matches. That makes tampering immediately detectable.

security.question: What makes it web3-native?
security.answer: Certify leverages decentralized trust and blockchain immutability so certification can be validated across organizations without relying on a single centralized authority. It makes the whole process more resilient and transparent.

security.question: How does Certify protect against fraud?
security.answer: Our multi-layer security approach uses cryptographic hashing, blockchain immutability, and distributed verification to eliminate counterfeiting and fraudulent credentials. Every check is independent, making fraud much harder to slip through.

security.question: Is Certify compliant with data protection regulations?
security.answer: Yes. Certify is designed to comply with GDPR, CCPA, and other data protection standards through encrypted processing, minimal data storage, and user-controlled verification. That lets organizations meet privacy requirements while still offering fast credential checks.

verification.question: How fast is the verification?
verification.answer: Verifications happen instantly with our optimized query flow, giving users immediate confirmation whether the certificate is authentic. Most checks complete in seconds, so users don't need to wait.

verification.question: Can I verify with QR or ID?
verification.answer: Yes. Certify is designed for multiple verification flows, including QR scanning, manual ID/hash checking, and future PDF validations. This flexibility fits both digital and print workflows.

verification.question: Who can use Certify?
verification.answer: Students, issuers, employers, and auditors can use Certify to validate credentials quickly and trust the results with blockchain-backed proof. It works well for hiring, admissions, and background checks.

verification.question: Can third parties verify certificates on my behalf?
verification.answer: Absolutely. Employers, educational institutions, and government agencies can verify any Certify certificate independently without needing the certificate holder's permission. That makes it easy to confirm credentials at scale.

verification.question: What happens if verification fails?
verification.answer: If a certificate fails verification, the system will provide a detailed report explaining why the credential could not be validated, helping identify potential issues or forgeries. This makes it easier to resolve mistakes or detect fraud quickly.

privacy.title: Privacy Policy
privacy.intro: At Certify, your privacy is a priority. Please read this policy carefully to understand how we handle your data when you use our blockchain-powered certificate verification platform.

sections.title: 1. Introduction
sections.content: Welcome to Certify. We are committed to protecting your personal information and your right to privacy. This Privacy Policy explains how we collect, use, disclose, and safeguard your information when you visit our platform and use our blockchain-based certificate verification services.

sections.title: 2. Information We Collect
sections.content: We may collect information that you voluntarily provide when using our services, including your name, email address, and certificate-related data submitted for verification. We also automatically collect certain technical data such as IP address, browser type, and usage patterns to improve platform performance.

sections.title: 3. How We Use Your Information
sections.content: We use the information we collect to provide, operate, and improve our services; process certificate verifications on the blockchain; communicate with you regarding your requests; ensure the security and integrity of the platform; and comply with applicable legal obligations.

sections.title: 4. Blockchain & On-Chain Data
sections.content: Certify utilizes blockchain technology for certificate verification. Only cryptographic proof hashes and minimal verification metadata are stored on-chain. No personally identifiable information (PII) is written to the public blockchain. All sensitive data remains encrypted and off-chain.

sections.title: 5. Sharing Your Information
sections.content: We do not sell, trade, or rent your personal information to third parties. We may share data with trusted service providers who assist in operating our platform, provided they agree to keep this information confidential. We may also disclose information when required by law or to protect our rights.

sections.title: 6. Data Security
sections.content: We implement industry-standard security measures including AES-256 encryption, secure HTTPS connections, and regular security audits to protect your information. While we strive to protect your data, no method of transmission over the internet is 100% secure.

sections.title: 7. Cookies & Tracking
sections.content: We use cookies and similar tracking technologies to enhance your experience on our platform. These help us remember your preferences and analyze how our service is used. You may disable cookies through your browser settings, though some features may not function properly as a result.

sections.title: 8. Your Rights
sections.content: Depending on your location, you may have certain rights regarding your personal data, including the right to access, correct, or delete your information; the right to data portability; and the right to object to or restrict processing. To exercise these rights, please contact us at the email below.

sections.title: 9. Third-Party Links
sections.content: Our platform may contain links to third-party websites. We are not responsible for the privacy practices or content of those sites. We encourage you to review the privacy policies of any third-party sites you visit.

sections.title: 10. Changes to This Policy
sections.content: We may update this Privacy Policy from time to time. We will notify you of any significant changes by updating the 'Last Updated' date at the top of this page. Continued use of the platform after changes constitutes your acceptance of the updated policy.

sections.title: 11. Contact Us
sections.content: If you have any questions or concerns about this Privacy Policy or our data practices, please contact us at: hello@certify.io — We aim to respond to all inquiries within 48 hours.

terms.title: Terms of Service
terms.intro: Please read these Terms of Service carefully before using Certify. By accessing our platform, you agree to comply with and be bound by the following terms and conditions governing your use of our blockchain-powered certificate verification services.

sections.title: 1. Acceptance of Terms
sections.content: By accessing or using Certify's platform and services, you agree to be bound by these Terms of Service. If you do not agree to these terms, please do not use our services. These terms apply to all users, including visitors, registered users, and certificate issuers.

sections.title: 2. Description of Services
sections.content: Certify provides a blockchain-based certificate verification platform that allows institutions to register credentials and individuals or organizations to verify their authenticity. Our services include manual ID-based verification, QR code scanning, and PDF validation tools.

sections.title: 3. User Accounts & Registration
sections.content: To access certain features of the platform, you may be required to create an account. You are responsible for maintaining the confidentiality of your account credentials and for all activities that occur under your account. You agree to provide accurate and complete information during registration.

sections.title: 4. Acceptable Use
sections.content: You agree to use Certify only for lawful purposes. You must not attempt to submit fraudulent certificate data, reverse-engineer the platform, perform unauthorized access to our systems, or use the service in any way that violates applicable laws or regulations.

sections.title: 5. Certificate Data & Accuracy
sections.content: Certify acts as a verification platform only. The accuracy and legitimacy of certificate data submitted by issuing institutions are the sole responsibility of those institutions. Certify does not independently verify the original credentials submitted by issuers.

sections.title: 6. Blockchain Immutability
sections.content: Once certificate proof data is recorded on the blockchain, it cannot be altered or deleted. Users and institutions should ensure all submitted data is accurate before initiating on-chain registration. Certify is not liable for errors in data submitted by third parties.

sections.title: 7. Intellectual Property
sections.content: All content, trademarks, logos, and software on the Certify platform are owned by or licensed to Certify. You may not reproduce, distribute, or create derivative works from any part of the platform without prior written consent from Certify.

sections.title: 8. Limitation of Liability
sections.content: To the maximum extent permitted by law, Certify shall not be liable for any indirect, incidental, special, or consequential damages arising out of or in connection with your use of the platform. Our total liability for direct damages shall not exceed the fees paid by you in the twelve months preceding the claim.

sections.title: 9. Disclaimer of Warranties
sections.content: Certify provides its services on an 'as is' and 'as available' basis without warranties of any kind, either express or implied. We do not warrant that the service will be uninterrupted, error-free, or free of harmful components.

sections.title: 10. Termination
sections.content: We reserve the right to suspend or terminate your access to the platform at any time, with or without notice, if you violate these Terms of Service or engage in conduct that we determine to be harmful to other users or the integrity of the platform.

sections.title: 11. Governing Law
sections.content: These Terms of Service shall be governed by and construed in accordance with the laws of the Republic of Indonesia, without regard to conflict of law principles. Any disputes arising under these terms shall be resolved in the courts of the applicable jurisdiction.

sections.title: 12. Changes to Terms
sections.content: Certify reserves the right to update these Terms of Service at any time. We will notify users of material changes by updating the 'Last Updated' date. Continued use of the platform after such changes constitutes your acceptance of the revised terms.

sections.title: 13. Contact Us
sections.content: If you have any questions about these Terms of Service, please contact us at: hello@certify.io — We aim to respond to all inquiries within 48 hours.

whyblockchain.title: Why Certify Uses Blockchain
whyblockchain.subtitle: and why we chose Polygon as our industrial and academic trust infrastructure.

conviction.title: Our Conviction
conviction.p1: At Certify, we have a clear conviction: a digital certificate must be independently, simply, and reliably verifiable — today and in the future.
conviction.p2: In a world where digital documents can be easily copied, altered, or forged, traditional certification based solely on PDF files or private databases is no longer sufficient. For this reason, Certify incorporates blockchain technology as a public verification record, carefully selected and applied with institutional judgment.

problem.title: The problem with traditional certificates
problem.subtitle: Today, millions of certificates circulate in digital format sharing a fundamental weakness: there is no simple, independent way to verify they haven't been altered.

items.title: Certification can be edited without leaving a trace
items.desc: PDFs and digital files can be easily edited or falsified with no visible trace.

items.title: Verification links often stop working
items.desc: School database validation links decay or break over time when web domains expire.

items.title: The issuing institution must be contacted manually
items.desc: Manual verifications via emails or phone calls waste hours of administrative labor.

items.title: The system depends entirely on a private database
items.desc: Private database hacks or crashes can corrupt all validation records permanently.

items.title: Legal uncertainty about file integrity
items.desc: Without public digital signatures, proving original integrity in court is extremely costly.

benefits.title: What blockchain brings to certification
benefits.subtitle: Blockchain solves this problem elegantly and robustly. At Certify, the process works like this:

cards.title: Unique Fingerprint
cards.description: A cryptographic fingerprint is generated that changes completely if even a single character in the document is altered.

cards.title: Public Record
cards.description: That fingerprint is permanently and immutably recorded on a decentralized, public blockchain network.

cards.title: Verification
cards.description: Anyone can verify the existence, timestamp, and immutability of the record using standard public on-chain tools.

benefits.fundamentalText: Certify does not store documents or personal data on the blockchain. The blockchain only contains a technical proof of integrity, protecting the holder's privacy.

suitability.title: Not all blockchains are suitable
suitability.subtitle: For Certify, a certification infrastructure must meet very specific conditions to be considered responsible and professional:

whyNotEth.title: Why not Ethereum?
whyNotEth.description: Ethereum is the reference standard, but its high operating costs (gas fees) make scalability difficult in educational and institutional contexts. It is not the most responsible option for an accessible, low-barrier, and time-sustainable service.

whyNotNew.title: Why not new networks?
whyNotNew.description: Experimental networks present risks of instability, centralization, and lesser institutional recognition. In certification, long-term trust and permanence outweigh trendy technological novelty.

whyPolygon.title: Why Certify Chose Polygon
whyPolygon.subtitle: Polygon represents the perfect equilibrium between decentralization, high speed, recognized standards, and minimal costs.

items.title: Transparent verification
items.description: Anyone can verify a Certify record using standard public explorers like Polygonscan, bypassing any proprietary middleman.

items.title: Sustainable costs
items.description: Allows registering thousands of certifications at micro-cent transaction costs, making the service accessible to all institutions.

items.title: Recognized standards
items.description: Being fully compatible with the Ethereum Virtual Machine (EVM), it uses formats widely accepted in international auditing and compliance.

items.title: Institutional reputation
items.description: Trusted and used by multiple global enterprises and governments, conveying unparalleled seriousness and technical stability.

items.title: Long-term vision
items.description: Has an incredibly active developer community, deep liquidity, and a technological roadmap designed to last for decades.

commitment.quote: "Certify uses blockchain as a public verification record, not as a tech trend or a marketing argument."
commitment.subtext: Those who use Certify can rest assured that their certificates are backed by a public, verifiable infrastructure designed to remain trustworthy over time. Polygon officially represents the right balance between trust, stability, and efficiency.
